title: Advanced Power BI
description: Elevate your Power BI skills. Master advanced DAX calculations, complex data modeling, dynamic row-level security, and performance optimization techniques for enterprise reporting.

seo_metadata.title: Advanced Power BI Certificate Course | ProCerix
seo_metadata.description: Master advanced Power BI and DAX. Learn complex data modeling, Time Intelligence, Row-Level Security, and optimization.

learning_outcomes: Write complex DAX functions, including CALCULATE and Time Intelligence
Design optimized Star Schema data models
Use parameters and variables in DAX
Optimize Power BI reports for faster load times

modules.title: Advanced Data Modeling
modules.description: Techniques for structuring data for optimal performance.

lessons.title: Star Schema Design
lessons.content:
Optimizing for Power BI

The Star Schema (Fact tables surrounded by Dimension tables) is the optimal design for Power BI. Learn how to denormalize data appropriately to achieve this structure.

lessons.title: Handling Many-to-Many Relationships
lessons.content:
Bridge Tables

Direct Many-to-Many relationships can cause ambiguous filter contexts. Learn to resolve this using a bridge table to ensure accurate DAX results.

modules.title: Mastering DAX
modules.description: Deep dive into the formula language.

lessons.title: The CALCULATE Function
lessons.content:
The Most Powerful Function

CALCULATE evaluates an expression in a modified filter context. It is essential for overriding default chart filters to calculate percentages or running totals.

lessons.title: Time Intelligence
lessons.content:
Date-Based Calculations

Functions like SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR, YTD, and DATEADD allow you to easily compare current performance against historical data. A dedicated Date table is required.

lessons.title: Row-Level Security (RLS)
lessons.content:
Restricting Data Access

RLS allows you to publish a single report but restrict the data users see based on their role (e.g., a regional manager only sees their region). This is configured in Desktop and managed in the Service.

lessons.title: Performance Analyzer
lessons.content:
Optimizing Reports

Use the built-in Performance Analyzer to identify slow-loading visuals or inefficient DAX queries, ensuring your enterprise dashboards load instantly.

mcqs.question: Which data model design is highly recommended for Power BI?
Which DAX function is used to evaluate an expression in a modified filter context?
What is required in your data model to use DAX Time Intelligence functions properly?

options.content: A direct connection to SQL Server

mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of Row-Level Security (RLS)?

options.content: To prevent users from editing DAX
To restrict data access for given users based on filters
To hide specific columns from everyone

mcqs.question: How should you resolve a Many-to-Many relationship in a robust data model?

options.content: Enable bi-directional filtering on all tables

mcqs.question: Which tool inside Power BI Desktop helps identify slow-loading visuals?
In a Star Schema, what type of table contains the quantitative data (numbers to aggregate)?
Which DAX function returns a table that contains a column of dates shifted either forward or backward in time?
When defining RLS roles in Power BI Desktop, you apply the DAX filter expression to:

options.content: A table in the data model

mcqs.question: Why is it recommended to use variables (VAR) in complex DAX measures?

options.content: They improve readability and can boost performance by caching intermediate results
They allow you to change the underlying SQL database
They are required by the CALCULATE function
They automatically format the output as currency
